name: split-usetimelinedata-ts-678-20260325-2047
idea: Split useTimelineData.ts (678 LOC) — the last god hook in the video editor. It mixes 6 concerns: (1) Data fetching/queries (timelineQuery, assetRegistryQuery, saveMutation) ~60 lines. (2) Save/sync lifecycle (saveTimeline, scheduleSave, commitData, applyTimelineEdit, applyResolvedConfigEdit, patchRegistry, editSeqRef, savedSeqRef, saveTimer, lastSavedSignature) ~200 lines. (3) Render state (renderStatus, renderProgress, renderLog, renderDirty, renderResult*, useClientRender wiring) ~60 lines. (4) UI preferences (scaleWidth, activeClipTab, assetPanel state, setters) ~50 lines. (5) Derived/memoized data (resolvedConfig, selectedClip, selectedTrack, compositionSize, renderMetadata, trackScaleMap) ~100 lines. (6) Asset operations (uploadAsset, registerAsset, uploadFiles, invalidateAssetRegistry, resolveAssetUrl, reloadFromServer) ~50 lines. Goal: each concern in its own hook under 200 LOC, useTimelineData becomes a thin orchestrator under 150 LOC that composes them. No behavioral changes — pure extraction.

last_gate.rationale: The three correctness flags from iteration 1 are all resolved with explicit wiring strategies. The remaining flag (`save-hook-loc-budget-not-feasible`) is an executor-level sizing concern, not a planning flaw. The plan already contains its own fallback: extract the two pure helpers to `lib/timeline-save-utils.ts` if the hook exceeds 200 LOC. A pure-function utility file is not a "new hook" — it's a leaf dependency with no state, no React, and no coupling concerns. Iterating further to pre-resolve a LOC estimate would be over-planning; the executor can make the call at extraction time.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Weighted score dropped sharply from 4.75 to 0.5 after all three correctness flags were resolved. Plan delta was 51.3% — large but justified since the revisions were targeted at specific wiring gaps, not structural rework. No recurring critiques across iterations. The single remaining flag is maintainability/sizing, not correctness. Preflight clean.
warnings: useTimelineSave will likely exceed 200 LOC. The executor should proactively extract shouldAcceptPolledData and buildDataFromCurrentRegistry to lib/timeline-save-utils.ts rather than trying to squeeze them in. Update the success criterion to allow one utility file for pure functions.
The 51.3% plan delta between v1 and v2 is large — the executor should read the v2 plan carefully rather than assuming familiarity from v1.

settled_decisions.decision: reloadFromServer lives in useTimelineSave, not useAssetOperations.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 1 gate review. The function is a save/sync reset path that touches editSeqRef, savedSeqRef, commitData, and saveStatus.

settled_decisions.decision: setRenderDirty is wired via onSaveSuccessRef (ref-based callback) from the orchestrator.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 1 gate review. Avoids coupling save and render hooks while preserving the save→render-dirty signal.

settled_decisions.decision: configSignatureRef and stableResolvedConfigRef move into useDerivedTimeline.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 1 gate review. Required for referential stability that RemotionPreview depends on.

settled_decisions.decision: A pure-function utility file (lib/timeline-save-utils.ts) is acceptable if useTimelineSave exceeds 200 LOC. This does not violate the 'no unnecessary abstractions' criterion since it contains only stateless pure functions.
settled_decisions.rationale: The save hook is the largest concern (~200 LOC). Allowing the executor to shed pure helpers prevents gaming the LOC target by cramming code.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
